free_text: Cross sections for proton-induced reactions on natSb
up to 68 MeV
Measurements were made at the 76-inch
cyclotron at the Crocker Nuclear Laboratory at the
University of California at Davis
Target stack, consisting of a series of
Kapton-covered foils of Sb, Al, and Cu. Between each
set of foils, an Al degrader was used to decrease the
beam energy.
After a 3 h cooling period, the foil stack was
disassembled, and the individual foils were counted
on a high purity germanium detector
Activation method was used.
used at EN=68 MeV
used at EN=68 MeV
used at EN=68 and 35 MeV
used at EN=35 MeV
